chunks.text: On Sunday afternoon, my mother asked me to do some shopping
so I went to the shopping street with my sister.
In my wallet, I had some money.
The biggest bread was a little expensive, so I chose a cheaper, smaller one.
The shop owner was a kind old lady.
When I paid, I took out a silver coin and a bill from my wallet.
Next, my sister went to the flower shop she loves.
Each flower had its own price tag.
“ Can I spend a little of my allowance?
I nodded, and she bought the flower she liked the most.
At the end of the street, there is a used bookshop.
The owner laughed and said, “ This shop sells books to many people.
On the way home, my sister said
“ I want to save a little of my money, not spend all of it.
” Using money carefully is not about being rich or poor.
